## Analysis Summary

I reviewed the CSV renderer implementation for issue 2b185002-d648-4a01-b926-a50f82e8f85e. All tests pass (178/178), including 5 CSV-specific tests and 13 DocumentViewer integration tests.

### Acceptance Criteria Verification:

**✅ Dedicated rich CSV table by default**
- Verified in `/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/web/src/components/Document/renderers/index.ts:76-87`
- CSV renderer registered with `match` function checking `.csv` extension
- Returns dedicated `CsvRenderer` component instead of fallback

**✅ Sortable columns**
- Verified in `/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/web/src/components/Document/renderers/CsvRenderer.tsx:103-114`
- Click handler toggles sort direction (asc/desc)
- Test coverage at `CsvRenderer.test.tsx:42-57`

**✅ Sticky header**
- Verified in `/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/web/src/components/Document/renderers/CsvRenderer.tsx:160-170`
- `position: 'sticky'` with `top: 0` and `zIndex: 1`
- Test coverage at `CsvRenderer.test.tsx:79-91`

**✅ Inline raw/rich toggle**
- Verified in `/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/web/src/components/Document/renderers/index.ts:82`
- `supportsRawToggle: true` capability declared
- DocumentViewer provides toggle buttons (line 140)

**✅ Search highlighting**
- Verified in `/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/web/src/components/Document/renderers/CsvRenderer.tsx:16-51`
- `renderHighlightedText` function with `<mark>` tags
- Test coverage at `CsvRenderer.test.tsx:59-77`

**✅ Large-file capped rich preview with full raw view**
- Verified in `/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/web/src/components/Document/renderers/CsvRenderer.helpers.ts:15-43`
- `buildCsvPreviewState` caps at 200 rows
- `buildCsvPreviewContent` provides capped content
- Raw view bypasses cap (DocumentViewer.tsx:144-147)
- Integration test at `DocumentViewer.test.tsx:287-308`

### Prior Failed Review Items:

**✅ CSV preview capping for multiline quoted CSV rows**
- Verified in `/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/web/src/components/Document/renderers/CsvRenderer.helpers.ts:7-12, 27-42`
- Uses `Papa.parse` which correctly handles quoted multiline cells
- Caps by **parsed rows** (not text lines), preserving cell integrity
- Test coverage at `CsvRenderer.test.tsx:93-115` explicitly verifies multiline cells aren't broken

**✅ Renderer-specific capped-preview coverage**
- Verified in `/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/web/src/components/Document/renderers/CsvRenderer.test.tsx:93-115`
- Test "builds a capped preview on full CSV rows without breaking multiline cells"
- Directly tests `buildCsvPreviewState` and `buildCsvPreviewContent` helpers
- Confirms preview state has `kind: 'rows'` and proper capping behavior

No significant issues found in the reviewed changes.I’ve confirmed the prior review findings were addressed in the code; I’m doing one last pass on repo/web quality signals before issuing the verdict.

The Rust tests and Node tests both pass successfully. The validation and linting commands encounter sandbox permission restrictions and cannot execute in this environment.## Acceptance criteria

**Pass.** The current change set now satisfies the issue’s stated CSV-renderer requirements.

- **Dedicated rich CSV view by default:** `web/src/components/Document/renderers/index.ts:75-87` registers a `csv` renderer matched by `.csv` extension and dispatches to `CsvRenderer` instead of the raw text/code path.
- **Sortable columns:** `web/src/components/Document/renderers/CsvRenderer.tsx:103-114,149-158` derives sorted rows from `sortState` and toggles ascending/descending order from header clicks.
- **Sticky header:** `web/src/components/Document/renderers/CsvRenderer.tsx:160-170` applies `position: 'sticky'` and `top: 0` to `<th>` cells.
- **Inline rich/raw switching:** `web/src/components/Document/renderers/index.ts:79-84` enables `supportsRawToggle`, and `web/src/components/Document/DocumentViewer.tsx:173-181,228-252` renders the raw/rich toggle and switches between capped rich content and full raw content.
- **Search highlighting in rich CSV view:** `web/src/components/Document/renderers/CsvRenderer.tsx:16-51,196-200` highlights matching cell fragments with `<mark>` nodes when search highlighting is active.
- **Large-file capped rich preview with full raw content preserved:** `web/src/components/Document/renderers/CsvRenderer.helpers.ts:15-43` now computes preview state by parsed CSV rows and rebuilds a capped CSV preview with `Papa.unparse`, avoiding truncation by raw newline count.

## Prior review feedback

**Resolved.**

- The earlier multiline-row corruption bug is fixed in `web/src/components/Document/renderers/CsvRenderer.helpers.ts:15-43`. Preview capping now happens on parsed rows via `parseCsv()` and `buildCsvPreviewContent()`, so quoted embedded newlines no longer let the viewer cut through a CSV record.
- Renderer-specific capped-preview coverage was added in `web/src/components/Document/renderers/CsvRenderer.test.tsx:93-115`. That test exercises `buildCsvPreviewState()` and `buildCsvPreviewContent()` directly and verifies multiline-cell preservation plus row-based capping.

## Architecture and separation of concerns

**Pass.** The change remains in the Web UI renderer layer.

- Renderer selection/capabilities stay in `web/src/components/Document/renderers/index.ts:31-48,63-110`.
- CSV parsing/preview logic is isolated to `web/src/components/Document/renderers/CsvRenderer.helpers.ts:7-43`.
- Presentation logic stays in `web/src/components/Document/renderers/CsvRenderer.tsx:66-212`.
- `web/src/components/Document/DocumentViewer.tsx:139-148` only orchestrates renderer capabilities and delegates CSV-specific preview handling through `getPreviewState` / `prepareRichContent`.

I did not find any domain/storage/command boundary violations.

## Functional paradigm

**Pass.** The implementation follows the existing React/functional style.

- Derived state is computed with `useMemo` in `CsvRenderer.tsx:85-114`.
- Parsing and preview shaping are pure helpers in `CsvRenderer.helpers.ts:7-43`.
- Row/header generation uses iterator-based transformations in `CsvRenderer.tsx:87-99,184-202` rather than imperative accumulation.

## Correctness and invariants

**Pass.**

- The previous correctness issue is addressed by row-based preview capping in `CsvRenderer.helpers.ts:15-43`.
- The dependency listed in the issue context is already complete, and this change builds directly on the renderer contract extension through `index.ts:43-47,85-86`.
- No Rust-side invariants in the review checklist (atomic writes, event logging, gate semantics, PID safety, unsafe code) are affected by this Web-only change set.

## Safety

**Pass.** No unsafe Rust is introduced; the reviewed files are TypeScript/React only.

## Testing

**Pass.**

- Renderer-specific tests cover parsing/rendering/sorting/highlighting/sticky header/capped preview in `web/src/components/Document/renderers/CsvRenderer.test.tsx:27-115`.
- Viewer-level tests cover raw/rich switching and capped-preview notice/full raw access in `web/src/components/Document/DocumentViewer.test.tsx:239-308`.
- Current runnable suites passed in this environment: `cargo test --workspace --quiet` and `web` `npm test -- --run`.
- I could not rerun `./scripts/jit-validate.sh` or `web` `npm run lint` due environment permission restrictions; for those, the latest available issue run history remains the best signal, and the prior recorded review output reported `./scripts/jit-validate.sh` passing.

## Error handling

**Pass.** I did not find new silent-failure or broad-catch debt in the CSV renderer changes. The helper/parser path is straightforward and consistent with the surrounding frontend code.

## CLI contract

**Pass / N/A.** No CLI surface changed.

## Documentation

**Pass / N/A.** No public Rust API was added or modified.

VERDICT: PASS
